rows.description: The first two letters on the License Plates indicate the region the plate was issued. Unlike Brazil, if you zoom out on the map, you cant find the two letter region code.

rows.indicator: white and yellow and black road markings
rows.description: India uses white dividers and has these yellow and black striped curbs / barricades in some major cities.
You can check out this Indian Language Comparison. page to get a better understanding of what the characters look like.
There is an entire established meta for regions in India based loosely around the shape / style of electricity pole you can find in the region.
Like most countries, you will find a lot of mobile numbers. There are still some cases where a landline number will be found and that can help you pinpoint a region.

India’s northern border is the Himalayan mountains. You will have VERY large mountains in some spots. These are known as the Palmyra palm, and found mostly on the eastern coast. You can find them anywhere from Sri Lanka all the way to Bangladesh. These palms are known as the Areca Palm and are usually found in eastern India surrounding Bangladesh, and specifically around Assam.
Its the pinpointing that may be a bit more difficult.

rows.indicator: pole topper in Punjab has a window shape under the central insulator
rows.description: The pole topper in Punjab has a window shape under the central insulator. This pole is located in Sikkim and is a mesh design. The poles in Karnataka have this trident design. It is not exclusive to this region, but it very common here. The poles in Tamil Nadu often have poles where the middle insulator is the same height as the two side insulators. Tamil Nadu also will have poles with this black and white painted pattern.

rows.indicator: poles in Kerala will have this round yellow sticker
rows.description: The poles in Kerala will have this round yellow sticker.

rows.indicator: This is the “Regular” India pole and the most common in the country
rows.description: This is the “Regular” India pole and the most common in the country. This pole is located mostly in Gujarat. It has holes in the side like Poland or Hungary. This pole is located in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh. It has indents in the concrete like Brazil and Chile. This pole can be found in Chattisgarh and has an additional bar making a trapezoid shape near the top.

warnings: Local deterministic extraction used GeoTips text only; associated images were not interpreted, so descriptions contain no image-derived details.
